      <title>Jaden Ivey Waived By Bulls After Anti-LGTBQ Comments</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284792/Jaden-Ivey-Waived-By-Bulls-After-Anti-LGTBQ-Comments</link>
      <description><p>Jaden Ivey will be waived by the Chicago Bulls following his recent anti-LGBTQ comments on social media. </p>
<p>The Bulls acquired Ivey from the Detroit Pistons ahead of the trade deadline and was set to be a restricted free agent in the offseason. Ivey was recently shut down for the season by the Bulls with a knee injury. </p>
<p>"The world proclaims LGBTQ, right? They proclaim Pride Month and the NBA does too. They show it to the world. They say, 'Come join us for Pride Month to celebrate unrighteousness,'" said Ivey. </p>
<p>Ivey went on Instagram Live, where he continued to talk about religion and the NBA.</p>
<p>"They're liars, bro. This is lying," Ivey said. "They're lying saying my conduct is detrimental to the team. That's a lie. Ask any one of them coaches in there, 'Was I a good teammate?' All I'm preaching about is Jesus Christ and they waived me. They say I'm crazy, right? I'm psycho."</p>
<p>He later said, "I didn't get myself waived. I was in the gym today. I was rehabbing, doing what was required of my job."</p>
<p>Billy Donovan responded to the decision to part ways with Ivey on Monday.</p>
<p>"I think there's a certain level of standards and expectations that are here," Donovan said. "I mean, we have people from all different walks of life working in the building and players from all different walks of life, right? So, the first thing is, everybody comes with their own personal experiences. But one is, we've got to all be professional. I think there's got to be a high level of respect for one another, and we got to help each other and then be accountable to those standards."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 30 Mar 2026 17:33:57 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284792/Jaden-Ivey-Waived-By-Bulls-After-Anti-LGTBQ-Comments</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Ivey_Jaden_det_221114.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Warriors Made Determined Push for Kawhi Leonard At Deadline, Expected To Revisit In Offseason</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284781/Warriors-Made-Determined-Push-for-Kawhi-Leonard-At-Deadline-Expected-To-Revisit-In-Offseason</link>
      <description><p>The Golden State Warriors made an aggressive late push for Los Angeles Clippers forward Kawhi Leonard in the final hour before the February 5 trade deadline, believing Leonard might become available after the Clippers agreed to send Ivica Zubac to Indiana. Los Angeles ultimately declined, but Golden State is expected to revisit the possibility this offseason, according to sources.</p>
<p>Leonard, who turns 35 in June, is having one of the finest seasons of his career, averaging 28.2 points, 6.3 rebounds, 3.6 assists and 1.9 steals with a PER of 28.0. Leonard is on track for a First or Second Team All-NBA selection if he reaches the 65-game minimum. He is signed for one more season at $50.3 million.</p>
<p>Multiple teams contacted the Clippers about Leonard at the deadline. Golden State was among the most determined pursuers, having also explored deals for Giannis Antetokounmpo and Jaren Jackson Jr. before ultimately acquiring Kristaps Porzingis from the Atlanta Hawks in a less ambitious move.</p>
<p>A potential Leonard-Golden State pairing carries some historical context. Leonard had interest in joining Jimmy Butler when both were free agents in 2019. Butler signed with the Miami Heat while Leonard landed in Los Angeles after the Clippers acquired Paul George. Butler, now with the Warriors, is expected to return from a torn ACL at some point during the 2026-27 season.</p>
<p>Whether the Clippers would entertain offers for Leonard this summer remains unclear. His combination of age, salary and one remaining contract year could make him an attractive but complicated trade target for any interested team.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 30 Mar 2026 12:24:42 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284781/Warriors-Made-Determined-Push-for-Kawhi-Leonard-At-Deadline-Expected-To-Revisit-In-Offseason</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Leonard_Kawhi_lac_250422.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Jayson Tatum, Nikola Jokic Named NBA's Players Of Week 23</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284790/Jayson-Tatum-Nikola-Jokic-Named-NBAs-Players-Of-Week-23</link>
      <description><p>Jayson Tatum and Nikola Jokic have been named the NBA's Players of the Week for Week 23.</p>
<p>Tatum averaged 25.7 points, 9.7 rebounds and 6.7 assists as the Boston Celtics went 3-0. Over 11 games since his return from an Achilles injury, Tatum is averaging 20.9 points, 9.1 rebounds and 4.2 assists with a PER of 18.7.</p>
<p>Jokic averaged 26.0 points, 17.0 rebounds and 14.0 assists as the Denver Nuggets went 4-0.</p></description>

      <title>UConn, South Carolina, Texas, UCLA All Reach Women's Final Four In 2nd Straight Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284794/UConn-South-Carolina-Texas-UCLA-All-Reach-Womens-Final-Four-In-2nd-Straight-Season</link>
      <description><p>The 2026 Women's Final Four is a complete repeat of the 2025 edition with UConn, UCLA, Texas and South Carolina all winning their respective regions as the No. 1 seeds. This is the second time that the identical four teams have reached the semifinals in consecutive seasons when UConn, Tennessee, Stanford and Georgia were in the Final Four in 1995 and 1996.</p>
<p>Geno Auriemma's UConn team has reached the Final Four in 17 out of the past 18 seasons, winning seven national championships over that timespan. </p>
<p>Dawn Staley's South Carolina has now been to six straight Final Fours, which is the longest streak in the country.</p>
<p>UConn and South Carolina, who will meet in the semifinals on Friday, have a 3-3 record against each other since 2021. The Huskies defeated South Carolina 82-59 in last year's national championship game.</p>
<p>Previously, South Carolina defeated UConn 64-49 in the 2022 national championship game.</p>
<p><img src="https://pbs.twimg.com/media/HEtTq04XcAA4Bik?format=jpg&name=medium" alt="" width="400" border="0" /></p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 30 Mar 2026 23:05:33 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284794/UConn-South-Carolina-Texas-UCLA-All-Reach-Womens-Final-Four-In-2nd-Straight-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_Connecticut.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Tom Dundon Group's $4.25 Billion Purchase Of Blazers Unanimously Approved By Board Of Governors</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284791/Tom-Dundon-Groups-$425-Billion-Purchase-Of-Blazers-Unanimously-Approved-By-Board-Of-Governors</link>
      <description><p>The NBA Board of Governors has unanimously approved the sale of the Portland Trail Blazers to a group led by Dallas businessman Tom Dundon, a league source told The Athletic. The deal is structured at an approximate total value of $4.25 billion.</p>
<p>The transaction ends the Allen family's ownership era dating to 1988. It is structured in two stages: an 80.1 percent stake closes March 31 at a $4 billion valuation, with the remaining 19.9 percent closing no later than September 1, 2028, at a $4.5 billion valuation. During the interim period, former Blazers chairman Bert Kolde will observe board meetings without governance authority.</p>
<p>Dundon's purchasing group includes Portland's Sheel Tyle of investment firm Collective Global, Chicago's Marc Zahr of Blue Owl, the Cherng family of Las Vegas, founders of Panda Express, and Freedom Mortgage CEO Stanley Middleman, who also holds a partial stake in the Philadelphia Phillies.</p>
<p>Dundon currently serves as majority owner of the NHL's Carolina Hurricanes, which he acquired in 2018 for $420 million. He recently sold 12.5 percent of that stake at a $2.66 billion valuation while retaining majority control. Carolina has reached the playoffs in eight straight seasons under his stewardship.</p>
<p>The Blazers are seeking $600 million to renovate the Moda Center. Oregon's Legislature has already committed $365 million, funded through income tax revenues from team-related activity. Additional contributions from Multnomah County and the city of Portland remain pending council votes.</p>
<p>Paul Allen purchased the franchise in 1988 for $70 million. Following his death in 2018, his sister Jody assumed governance. The Allen estate listed the team for sale in May 2025, with all proceeds designated for charitable distribution.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 30 Mar 2026 16:09:08 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284791/Tom-Dundon-Groups-$425-Billion-Purchase-Of-Blazers-Unanimously-Approved-By-Board-Of-Governors</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_portlandtrailblazers.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Bobby Portis Says Giannis Antetokounmpo Will Not Play Again This Season For Bucks</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284789/Bobby-Portis-Says-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Will-Not-Play-Again-This-Season-For-Bucks</link>
      <description><p>Milwaukee Bucks forward Bobby Portis publicly stated that teammate Giannis Antetokounmpo will not return to the court this season, citing both the team's eliminated playoff status and broader business considerations surrounding a potential trade.</p>
<p>Portis, entering his sixth season with Milwaukee, made the remarks during an appearance on FanDuel TV's "Run It Back." The Bucks were mathematically eliminated from postseason contention Saturday, sitting at 29-45 with eight games remaining.</p>
<p>"I don't think he'll play another game this year, for sure," Portis said. "Just being smart as a pro, right now, I don't think it's smart for him to play."</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo has suited up for just 36 games this season amid a string of injuries. He has missed the club's last seven contests following a left knee hyperextension. The Athletic previously reported he resisted the organization's suggestion to shut him down entirely.</p>
<p>A dispute over his status has intensified in recent days. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284657/NBPA-Critical-Of-Bucks-Injury-Management-Of-Giannis-Antetokounmpo">The National Basketball Players Association released a statement claiming Antetokounmpo is healthy and the Bucks are preventing his return</a>. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284688/Doc-Rivers-Pushes-Back-On-NBPA-Tanking-Claims-Says-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Simply-Not-Healthy">Doc Rivers directly contested that assertion</a>, maintaining the star has not received medical clearance.</p>
<p>Portis did not shy away from the trade speculation surrounding the two-time MVP.</p>
<p>"If y'all talking about trading him, you don't want any injury to knock value off or whatever," Portis said. "That's just business-wise, and then player-wise, obviously, you gotta look at just the total picture of what's going on. We're 10 games behind for the 10th seed and (with) nine games left, we can't even make up ground."</p>
<p>Bucks general manager Jon Horst fielded trade inquiries ahead of the February deadline before opting to retain Antetokounmpo. Renewed discussions are anticipated this offseason. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 30 Mar 2026 16:02:09 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284789/Bobby-Portis-Says-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Will-Not-Play-Again-This-Season-For-Bucks</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Antetokounmpo_Giannis_mil_250323.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Mark Cuban Regrets Selling Mavericks To Adelson/Dumont Family</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284788/Mark-Cuban-Regrets-Selling-Mavericks-To-Adelson-Dumont-Family</link>
      <description><p>Mark Cuban sold the Dallas Mavericks over two years ago and the franchise immediately went on to The Finals in 2024, but Nico Harrison's decision to trade Luka Doncic in February 2025 has created a tumultuous period for the franchise. Cuban was sidelined by Harrison, who reported directly to team governor Patrick Dumont.</p>
<p>Cuban has been increasingly outspoken about the Mavericks and this week has said he regrets selling the Mavericks to Miriam Adelson.</p>
<p>"I don't regret selling. I regret who I sold to," Cuban said in a teaser clip released Monday. "I made a lot of mistakes in the process, and I'll leave it at that."</p>
<p>Cuban, who has been one of the NBA's most emotionally invested owners, has said he didn't want to burden his children with the responsibilities of owning the Mavericks.</p>
<p>"I didn't want that for them. It can be abusive, a lot," Cuban said. "If fans don't like what you're doing or if the team's not doing well, you're the worst human being on the planet and they treat you that way."</p>
<p>The Mavericks were given a lifeline to recover from the fallout of trading Doncic when they won the lottery last May and subsequently selected Cooper Flagg.</p>
<p>Harrison was fired by the Mavericks in November and the front office is currently being run by Michael Finley and Matt Riccardi on an interim basis. When Cuban sold the Mavericks, <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/273835/Mark-Cuban-To-Control-Mavs-Basketball-Operations-Forevermore">he had expected to run basketball operations "forevermore</a>."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 30 Mar 2026 15:09:29 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284788/Mark-Cuban-Regrets-Selling-Mavericks-To-Adelson-Dumont-Family</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Cuban_Mark_dal_180221.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Kevin Pelton Hired By Houston Comets As Assistant GM, VP Of Analytics</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284787/Kevin-Pelton-Hired-By-Houston-Comets-As-Assistant-GM-VP-Of-Analytics</link>
      <description><p>ESPN's Kevin Pelton has been hired for a front office role of the Houston Comets, who are relocating from Connecticut ahead of the 2027-28 season. Pelton will be assistant general manager and vice president of analytics with the franchise.</p>
<p>Pelton will work with the Sun front office this season as they prepare for their offseason period. The Sun are run by general manager Morgan Tuck and team president Jen Rizzotti.</p>
<p>Pelton covered the Seattle Storm of the WNBA from 2003 until 2012. Pelton was a statistical consultant for the Indiana Pacers from 2010 until 2012 before joining ESPN in 2013. Pelton is perhaps best known for his trade and free agency grades.</p>
<p> </p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Jamhal Mosley Accepts Responsibility After Magic Surrender 31-0 Run, 52-Point Loss To Raptors</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284767/Jamhal-Mosley-Accepts-Responsibility-After-Magic-Surrender-31-0-Run-52-Point-Loss-To-Raptors</link>
      <description><p>Orlando Magic head coach Jamahl Mosley pointed blame at himself Sunday after his team suffered the most lopsided loss in franchise history, a 139-87 defeat to the Toronto Raptors that included a 31-0 run spanning 7 minutes and 47 seconds, which is the longest unanswered scoring surge in the NBA's play-by-play era.</p>
<p>"We've got to have them prepared for the physicality of the game, prepared for how much they were going to grab, hold and get them off their spots," Mosley said. "That's on me."</p>
<p>"I've got to be able to do a better job of preparing them for what they were going to see tonight," he added. "We talked a little bit about it, but probably not enough."</p>
<p>Orlando committed 28 turnovers, including 12 in the first quarter alone, tying a team record for most in any period. Toronto converted those miscues into 37 points and outscored the Magic 39-7 in fast-break points. The Magic trailed 70-43 at halftime.</p>
<p>The defeat was a significant missed opportunity. Toronto was without starting guard Immanuel Quickley, leading scorer Brandon Ingram and two rotation players, yet handled Orlando with ease. The Magic entered the game two games behind the fifth-place Raptors in a crowded Eastern Conference standings race.</p>
<p>Paolo Banchero declined to place blame solely on Mosley.</p>
<p>"It's all a collective," Banchero said. "Gotta find ways to stop runs like that. We didn't. If you want to be a good team, you can't give up runs like that."</p>
<p>Orlando has now dropped seven of eight games, including losses to the league-worst Indiana Pacers and the Charlotte Hornets. With eight games remaining, the Magic hold the eighth seed but risk falling to the Play-In Tournament's lower bracket.</p>
<p>Despite the historic defeat, Mosley expressed confidence his message was still connecting.</p>
<p>"You're not as bad as you think you are in your losses, and you're not as great as you think you are in your wins," he said. "We've got to make sure we get on the right mentality coming down to these last eight games."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 30 Mar 2026 07:27:37 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284767/Jamhal-Mosley-Accepts-Responsibility-After-Magic-Surrender-31-0-Run-52-Point-Loss-To-Raptors</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Mosley_Jamahl_orl_231205.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Joe Mazzulla Calls Coach Of The Year Award 'Dumb,' Credits Players, Staff With Surprise Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284793/Joe-Mazzulla-Calls-Coach-Of-The-Year-Award-Dumb-Credits-Players-Staff-With-Surprise-Season</link>
      <description><p>Boston Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla wants no part of Coach of the Year conversations, delivering a characteristically blunt assessment of the award Monday despite being considered one of the leading candidates.</p>
<p>"I don't need it," Mazzulla said. "I think it's a stupid award. They shouldn't have it. And it's more about the players. It's more about the work that the staff puts in. It's just that simple. I really don't ever want to be asked or talk about it again. It's just that dumb. So, the players play. It's about them. Staff works their ass off. I'm grateful to have them."</p>
<p>Mazzulla's candidacy is well founded regardless of his indifference. He has guided Boston to its fifth consecutive 50-win season through a significant offseason roster overhaul and Jayson Tatum missing most of the year with a torn Achilles. The Celtics currently sit second in the Eastern Conference.</p>
<p>Atlanta Hawks coach Quin Snyder offered an expansive endorsement of Mazzulla's work.</p>
<p>"Anytime your roster changes, when you lose a player of Jayson Tatum's caliber, you adjust," Snyder said. "What Joe's done is figure out a way to play that maximizes the personality that they have. And I think ultimately that's fundamentally, when I look at them and watch them play, why I would say that he's done such a terrific job."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 30 Mar 2026 22:20:45 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284793/Joe-Mazzulla-Calls-Coach-Of-The-Year-Award-Dumb-Credits-Players-Staff-With-Surprise-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Mazzulla_Joe_bos_240606.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Arizona, Michigan Co-Favorites At +175 To Win NCAA Tournament</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284786/Arizona-Michigan-Co-Favorites-At-+175-To-Win-NCAA-Tournament</link>
      <description><p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/ncaab/march-madness/odds-lines/">Arizona and Michigan enter the Final Four as co-favorites to win the NCAA men's basketball tournament at +175</a>, according to DraftKings. Illinois (+400) and UConn (+550) round out the field as long shots.</p>
<p>Both Arizona and Michigan entered March Madness with strong odds, +380 and +370, respectively. Illinois and UConn were far longer shots at 22-1 and 25-1 entering the tournament. Duke, the original favorite at +360, was eliminated on a buzzer-beating three-pointer by UConn.</p>
<p>One Caesars Sportsbook bettor in New Jersey had $300,000 riding on UConn pregame at +205 odds.</p>
<p>"We will be rooting for the winner of the Arizona-Michigan semifinal to take care of business in the final. We've spent all season trying to build a good position on these teams (and Duke, but that's another story), and hopefully it will pay off," Circa Sports race & sportsbook manager Chase Michaelson told ESPN. "I would consider Arizona or Michigan excellent results. Illinois is bad, UConn is worse."</p>
<p>BetMGM senior trader Michael Ranftle identified UConn as the book's sole remaining liability. The sportsbook accepted a $25,000 wager on UConn at +900 and a $50,000 wager on Arizona at +450, each worth $225,000 if cashed. FanDuel reports UConn has drawn the highest volume of championship bets overall and in the past 24 hours.</p>
<p>BetMGM's ideal outcome is a Michigan title, with Arizona or Illinois also favorable results. </p>
<p>"This is as close as it can get," Michaelson said. "These are two of the best teams we've seen in quite some time, in terms of a mix of NBA talent, college experience and coaching... In most years, either of these teams would likely be odds-on favorites to win the title by this time, but they have to play each other."</p>
<p>Michigan opened as a slight underdog but has since moved to -1.5 over Arizona. Illinois holds steady at -2.5 against UConn, though early ticket volume favors the Huskies.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 30 Mar 2026 14:12:17 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284786/Arizona-Michigan-Co-Favorites-At-+175-To-Win-NCAA-Tournament</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Lloyd_Tommy_az_260316.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Dunc'd On: 15 in 60 Young Player Progress Reports (Western Conference 3.30.26)</title>

      <description><p><iframe title="YouTube video player" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/c80x-4pwFAY?si=BlvnwP3LKejdC2Bp" width="560" height="315" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen="allowfullscreen" data-mce-fragment="1"></iframe></p>
<div class="episode-long-desc" data-v-abc7255a="" data-v-17ef938d="">
<p>Nate and Danny check in on the progression of every young player of note in the West</p>

<li>LAC: Bennedict Mathurin, Kobe Sanders, Cam Christie, Yanic Konan Niederhauser 42:39</li>

<li>MEM: Javon Small, Cedric Coward, Taylor Hendricks, GG Jackson, O-Max Prosper, Walter Clayton, Cam Spencer, Jahmai Mashack, Jaylen Wells, Zach Edey 52:18</li>
<li>MIN: Jaylen Clark, Terrence Shannon, Julian Phillips, Joan Beringer 1:13:11</li>

      <title>EuroLeague's Hire Of Former NBA Exec Chus Bueno As CEO Fuels Optimism Over NBA Merger</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284782/EuroLeagues-Hire-Of-Former-NBA-Exec-Chus-Bueno-As-CEO-Fuels-Optimism-Over-NBA-Merger</link>
      <description><p>The EuroLeague's appointment of Chus Bueno as its new CEO has generated fresh optimism among league sources that the NBA and EuroLeague can reach a collaborative arrangement rather than compete directly against one another in Europe. Bueno is a former NBA executive who worked on league development initiatives across Europe, Africa and the Middle East.</p>
<p>The shift in sentiment coincides with NBA Commissioner Adam Silver's most direct public statement yet on the league's preferred approach to European expansion, delivered Wednesday at the Board of Governors news conference.</p>
<p>"I think for the betterment of European basketball," Silver said, "the best outcome would be if we came together with the EuroLeague here and that we came up with a systematic approach to growing the game throughout Europe."</p>
<p>The NBA has been working toward launching its own European league targeting a 2027-28 start, with planned franchises in major cities across England, France, Germany, Italy and Spain. The external bid process for franchises is expected to close by month's end.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/international/league/1/euroleague">The EuroLeague currently includes 13 licensed shareholder clubs with long-term standing in the competition, which features 20 teams this season</a>. Any NBA Europe structure is expected to accommodate 12 locked-in franchises with four annual qualifying spots, meaning not all 13 existing EuroLeague clubs could be absorbed. Including established powers such as Real Madrid and Barcelona, however, would significantly strengthen any combined venture's commercial appeal and credibility across the continent.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 30 Mar 2026 12:30:02 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284782/EuroLeagues-Hire-Of-Former-NBA-Exec-Chus-Bueno-As-CEO-Fuels-Optimism-Over-NBA-Merger</guid>

      <title>St. Bonaventure Hires Mike MacDonald From Division II Daemen As Head Coach</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284780/St-Bonaventure-Hires-Mike-MacDonald-From-Division-II-Daemen-As-Head-Coach</link>
      <description><p>St. Bonaventure is finalizing a deal to hire Daemen College coach Mike MacDonald as its next men's basketball head coach, a source briefed on the decision confirmed. The announcement is expected this week, continuing the program's pattern of outside-the-box appointments following its 2024 hire of ESPN insider Adrian Wojnarowski as general manager.</p>
<p>MacDonald, 59, spent 12 seasons at Division II Daemen in Amherst, N.Y., compiling a 61-3 record over the past two years. This season's Daemen squad finished 33-2 before falling in the Division II Elite Eight.</p>
<p>He replaces Mark Schmidt, who retired after 19 seasons, a program-record 341 victories and three NCAA Tournament appearances. St. Bonaventure parted ways with Schmidt with one year left on his contract after the team stumbled to a 4-14 Atlantic 10 finish following an 11-2 start, a decision that drew criticism from fans who felt Schmidt deserved a more dignified departure.</p>
<p>Athletic director Bob Beretta acknowledged the controversy in a March 17 open letter, describing the program as being at an "inflection point" and calling for a leader capable of maximizing resources in the NIL era.</p>
<p>MacDonald is expected to be among the Atlantic 10's lowest-paid coaches, a structure that could allow St. Bonaventure to direct additional funds toward its roster to compete in the NIL era. The model pairs a low-profile coach with Wojnarowski, whose extensive NBA connections could prove valuable in recruiting.</p>
<p>Earlier in his career, MacDonald compiled a 108-153 record over nine seasons at Canisius, where he replaced John Beilein as head coach in 1997 after serving on his staff.</p>
<p>The hiring reflects a broader trend of Division II coaches earning opportunities at higher levels. Iowa's Ben McCollum, who won four national championships at Northwest Missouri State, has guided the Hawkeyes to the Elite Eight in his first season. Josh Schertz, who spent 13 years as a Division II head coach, led Saint Louis to the second round of this year's NCAA Tournament.</p></description>

   </item>

      <description><p>Portland Trail Blazers forward Jerami Grant will be out indefinitely due to a right calf injury. Grant said he felt a "pop" in his calf during Friday's loss to the Dallas Mavericks. The veteran forward had an MRI, but a timeline to return won't be determined until Grant undergoes more testing.</p>
<p>Grant has rebounded this season to be a productive contributor for Portland. On the season, the 12th-year veteran has averaged 18.6 points, 3.5 rebounds and 2.1 assists per game. Grant has been more efficient, as he's shot 45.3% from the field, including 38.9% on 6.1 three-point attempts per game.</p></description>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284771/Jaylen-Wells-Likely-To-Miss-Remainder-Of-Season-After-Toe-Surgery</link>
      <description><p>The Memphis Grizzlies injury-ravaged season has claimed another victim. Jaylen Wells will undergo surgery to repair a tendon avulsion fracture in his right big toe. While Memphis did not attach a timeline for Wells' return, it's likely he'll miss the remainder of the season.</p>
<p>In his sophomore season, Wells improved on the surprising success of his rookie season. The 6-foot-7 wing averaged 12.5 points, 3.2 rebounds and continued to play very good defense.</p>
<p>Wells joins six other Grizzlies as sidelined after season-ending injuries and/or surgeries. Santi Aldama, Kentavious Caldwell-Pope, Brandon Clarke, Zach Edey, Ja Morant and Scotty Pippen Jr. are all out for the remainder of the season.</p></description>

      <description><p>The Portland Trail Blazers and Golden State Warriors are now locked into spots in the Western Conference Play-In Tournament.       <title>Celtics Clinch 2026 NBA Playoffs Spot</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284769/Celtics-Clinch-2026-NBA-Playoffs-Spot</link>
      <description><p>The Boston Celtics became the second Eastern Conference team to clinch a spot in the 2026 NBA Playoffs. Boston joined the Detroit Pistons as the two teams to have clinched top-six spots in the playoffs.</p>
<p>Boston is currently second in the East, four games behind Detroit. The Celtics have a 2.5-game lead over the New York Knicks for the second seed in the Eastern Conference.</p>
<p>This is Boston's 12th consecutive playoff appearance, which is the longest current streak in the NBA. Overall, the Celtics have made the playoffs in 18 of the last 19 seasons and 22 of the last 25 seasons.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Jaylen Brown Calls Out 'Gap Year' Expectations As Celtics Win 50th Game</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284768/Jaylen-Brown-Calls-Out-Gap-Year-Expectations-As-Celtics-Win-50th-Game</link>
      <description><p>After the Boston Celtics clinched a playoff berth with a 114-99 victory over the Charlotte Hornets on Sunday, Jaylen Brown posted a simple message on social media in response to those who wrote off their chances of accomplishing anything this season amid injury and <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Boston-Celtics/2/roster-turnover">roster turnover</a>.</p>
<p>"50 wins in a gap year," Brown wrote.</p>
<p>The remark was a direct callback to preseason skepticism that followed Jayson Tatum's torn Achilles and the offseason trades of Jrue Holiday and Kristaps Porzingis. The Celtics also lost Luke Kornet and Al Horford to free agency. Boston has answered those doubts emphatically, reaching 50 wins for the fifth consecutive season and extending the NBA's longest active playoff streak to 12 years.</p>
<p>Brown sat out Sunday's game with left Achilles tendinitis, but Tatum carried the offense with 32 points, his highest total in 11 games since returning from injury earlier this month. The Celtics have gone 9-2 since Tatum's return and have won seven of their past eight games, including a victory over the league-best Oklahoma City Thunder.</p>
<p>Tatum's performance featured an array of precise footwork, decisive drives and patient playmaking against double-teams. He also became the youngest player in Celtics history to reach 14,000 career points.</p>
<p>"I didn't feel like I was sped up," Tatum said. "I was still kind of tired, so that's something I'm still working through. But I like that I'm being decisive in my movements and exploding when I need to. I can say I feel better than I did last game."</p>
<p>Tatum acknowledged the demanding nature of his rehabilitation process while expressing gratitude for his supporting cast.</p>
<p>"I've had to work my [tail] off just to run up and down the floor," he said. "I've been fortunate enough to be on some really good teams, be surrounded by some really good players and coaches that have empowered me and helped me become the player I am today."</p>
<p>With Brown leading what many consider an <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/ballot">MVP-caliber season</a> and Tatum trending upward, Boston has emerged as a legitimate championship contender heading into the postseason with the second best <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/nba-championship-betting-odds/">NBA championship betting odds</a> behind the Oklahoma City Thunder. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/nba-mvp-betting-odds/">Brown ranks fifth in MVP odds</a>, setting himself up for an excellent shot at being an All-NBA First Team selection.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 30 Mar 2026 07:34:40 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284768/Jaylen-Brown-Calls-Out-Gap-Year-Expectations-As-Celtics-Win-50th-Game</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Brown_Jaylen_bos_250504.jpg" />
   </item>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284766/Mavs-Have-Not-Hired-Search-Firm-For-New-Head-Of-Basketball-Operations</link>
      <description><p>The Dallas Mavericks have not hired a search firm to help find a new head of basketball operations, according to Marc Stein of The Stein Line.</p>
<p>The team was looking to fill the role by the draft in June.</p>
<p>Michael Finley or Matt Riccardi, who are currently co-interim GMs, could still receive promotions into the position.</p>
<p>It was reported earlier in the season that the Mavs will value previous experience as a lead NBA executive, with <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/283188/Mavericks-To-Keep-Interim-GMs-Through-Trade-Deadline-After-Nico-Harrison-Firing">Dennis Lindsey</a> mentioned as a possible candidate.  </p>
<p>Stein also notes that the team is also growing fond of Khris Middleton, who will be an unrestricted free agent this summer. </p></description>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284765/Kristaps-Porzingis-Expected-To-Re-Sign-With-Warriors</link>
      <description><p>Expectations are increasing that Kristaps Porzingis will re-sign with the Golden State Warriors this offseason, according to Marc Stein of The Stein Line.</p>
<p>Porzingis recently praised vice president of player health Rick Celebrini as the "GOAT," further leading to speculation that Porzingis will remain with the Warriors.</p>
<p>The Warriors acquired Porzingis, who will be an unrestricted free agent, at the trade deadline.</p>
<p>Porzingis is averaging 17.1 points in ten games with the Warriors.</p>
<p>The Warriors are also linked with <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284756/Warriors-Adding-LeBron-James-Has-Some-Legs">potentially signing LeBron James</a> this offseason.</p></description>
